perturbation.original_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) is duly amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) believes it is amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and it will use commercially reasonable efforts to remain up-to-date in respect of corporate filings and maintain good standing under such Act; (ii) believes it has the requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) has the corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder to the best of its understanding;
perturbation.explanation: By changing "is duly amalgamated" to "believes it is amalgamated", and "is up-to-date" to "will use commercially reasonable efforts to remain up-to-date", the obligation becomes ambiguous and weakens the certainty regarding the corporation's legal status and compliance. Also adding "to the best of its understanding" introduces subjectivity.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 257 of the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) mandates that corporations must be validly amalgamated and in good standing to conduct business legally. The modified text introduces ambiguity by using 'believes' and 'commercially reasonable efforts,' which fails to guarantee the absolute compliance required by the Act.

Incorporation 3.1 Professional co
perturbation.justification: The modified contract language weakens the certainty of the corporation's legal status and compliance, introducing ambiguity through the use of "believes" and "commercially reasonable efforts". The original text clearly states the corporation "is duly amalgamated" and "is up-to-date", providing a definitive statement of its legal standing. In contrast, the modified language creates an ambiguous obligation as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The Corporation will cause a Form D to be filed with the SEC within 15 days of the first sale of the Offered Shares to a U.S. Subscriber, and will, within the prescribed time periods, prepare and file any other forms or notices required under any state securities laws in connection with the offer and sale of Offered Shares.
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation will endeavor to file a Form D with the SEC shortly after the first sale of the Offered Shares to a U.S. Subscriber, and will attempt to prepare and file other forms or notices that it believes are required under state securities laws in connection with the offer and sale of Offered Shares.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'will cause' with 'will endeavor' and 'will prepare and file' with 'will attempt to prepare and file' weakens the obligation to file the required forms. Adding 'that it believes are required' introduces subjectivity, creating ambiguity as to whether all necessary filings will be completed accurately and on time.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 230.503 requires the timely filing of Form D with the SEC. By changing 'will cause' to 'will endeavor' and introducing subjectivity with 'that it believes are required,' the Corporation's obligation becomes ambiguous, and the altered text fails to guarantee the mandatory and timely filing of the form as required by the regulation.
perturbation.location: Schedule A, Representations, Warranties and Covenants of the Corporation, Section 9

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.503 - Filing of notice of sales. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.503 - Filing of notice of sales. CFR prev | next § 230.503 Filing of notice of sales. (a) When notice of sales on Form D is required and permitted to be filed. (1) An issuer offering or selling securities in reliance on § 230.504 or § 230.506 must file with the Commission a notice of sales containing the information required by Form D ( 17 CFR 239.500 ) for each new offering of securities no later than 15 calendar days after the first sale of securities in the offering, unless the end of that period falls on a Saturday, Sunday or holiday, in which case the due date would be the first business day following. (2) An issuer may file an amendment to a previously filed notice of sales on Form D at any time. (3) An issuer must file an amendment to a previously filed notice of sales on Form D for an offering: (i) To correct a material mistake of fact or error in the previously filed notice of sales on Form D, as soon as practicable after discovery of the mistake or error; (ii) To reflect a change in the information provided in the previously filed notice of sales on Form D, as soon as practicable after the change, except that no amendment is required to reflect a change that occurs after the offering terminates or a change that occurs solely in the following information: (A) The address or relationship to the issuer of a related person identified in response to Item 3 of the notice of sales on Form D; (B) An issuer 's revenues or aggregate net asset value; (C) The minimum investment amount , if the change is an increase, or if the change, together with all other changes in that amount since the previously filed notice of sales on Form D, does not result in a decrease of more than 10%; (D) Any address or state(s) of solicitation shown in response to Item 12 of the notice of sales on Form D; (
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 17 CFR § 230.503, which mandates the filing of Form D within 15 days of the first sale of securities. The original text states, 'The Corporation will cause a Form D to be filed,' indicating a firm obligation. The changed text, however, uses 'will endeavor to file,' which weakens the obligation and introduces uncertainty. Furthermore, adding 'that it believes are required' introduces subjectivity regarding which forms must be filed, contrary to the regulation's requirement for specific filings. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states, 'An issuer offering or selling securities in reliance on § 230.504 or § 230.506 must file with the Commission a notice of sales containing the information required by Form D... no later than 15 calendar days after the first sale of securities in the offering...' The altered text does not ensure this mandatory and timely filing, creating ambiguity and potential non-compliance.
